Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd. Revises Consolidated Earnings Guidance for the Fiscal Year Ending March 31, 2026
data.description:
Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd. revised consolidated earnings guidance for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. For the year, the company now expects net income attributable to the company of JPY 320,000 million compared to previous guidance of JPY 380,000 million and net income per share of JPY 109.65 compared to previous guidance of JPY 127.90 per share. Reason for the revision: The life insurance business segment is expected to see an increase in investment income due to an upturn in the investment environment, together with a decrease in business expenses.

The postal and domestic logistics business segment is expected to feel the impact on revenue and expenses of administrative dispositions associated with unperformed roll calls, in addition to slower-than-expected growth in parcel revenue.

data.title: Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd. Announces Dividend for the Second Quarter Ended September 30, 2025, Payable on December 5, 2025
data.description: Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd. announced dividend of JPY 25.00 per share for the second quarter ended September 30, 2025 compared to JPY 25.00 per share paid a year ago. Scheduled date of commencing dividend payments: December 5, 2025.

data.title: Melrose Industries plc Confirms Revenue Guidance for the Year 2025
data.description: Melrose Industries PLC confirms revenue guidance for the year 2025. For the period, the company expects Revenue range of £3.425 billion to £3.575 billion.

data.title: Airtac International Group Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Airtac International Group reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was TWD 8,245.91 million compared to TWD 7,380.79 million a year ago. Net income was TWD 2,099.47 million compared to TWD 1,732.37 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 10.5 compared to TWD 8.66 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 10.5 compared to TWD 8.66 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was TWD 25,294.81 million compared to TWD 23,014.32 million a year ago. Net income was TWD 6,072.28 million compared to TWD 5,822.65 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 30.36 compared to TWD 29.11 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was TWD 30.34 compared to TWD 29.1 a year ago.

data.title: Aboitiz Power Corporation Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Aboitiz Power Corporation reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was PHP 52,530.54 million compared to PHP 48,081.53 million a year ago. Net income was PHP 10,636.97 million compared to PHP 10,146.2 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was PHP 1.48 compared to PHP 0.4 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was PHP 1.48 compared to PHP 0.4 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was PHP 144,326.96 million compared to PHP 148,320.98 million a year ago. Net income was PHP 23,303.57 million compared to PHP 27,273.87 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was PHP 3.23 compared to PHP 3.78 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was PHP 3.23 compared to PHP 3.78 a year ago.

data.title: Oil India Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Oil India Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 91,754.1 million compared to INR 81,359 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 96,925.8 million compared to INR 88,868.9 million a year ago. Net income was INR 14,288.3 million compared to INR 20,162 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.78 compared to INR 12.4 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.78 compared to INR 12.4 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 8.78 compared to INR 12.4 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 8.78 compared to INR 12.4 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 179,253.5 million compared to INR 174,867.9 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 186,982 million compared to INR 184,686.7 million a year ago. Net income was INR 33,252.5 million compared to INR 39,019.8 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 20.44 compared to INR 23.99 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 20.44 compared to INR 23.99 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 20.44 compared to INR 23.99 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 20.44 compared to INR 23.99 a year ago.

data.title: Max Healthcare Institute Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Max Healthcare Institute Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 21,354.7 million compared to INR 17,074.6 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 21,683.7 million compared to INR 17,483 million a year ago. Net income was INR 4,913 million compared to INR 2,818.1 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 5.05 compared to INR 2.9 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 5.02 compared to INR 2.88 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 41,630.4 million compared to INR 32,504.1 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 42,324.5 million compared to INR 33,259.3 million a year ago. Net income was INR 7,992.7 million compared to INR 5,180.8 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.22 compared to INR 5.33 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.17 compared to INR 5.3 a year ago.

White House says Alibaba is helping Chinese military target US, FT reports
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -Washington is accusing online marketplace company Alibaba of providing technological support for Chinese military "operations" against targets in the United States, the Financial Times said on Friday, citing a White House national security memo.

The memo includes declassified "top secret" intelligence on how the Chinese group supplies the People's Liberation Army with capabilities that the White House believes threaten U.S. security, the FT reported.

Alibaba and the Chinese Embassy in Washington did not immediately respond to requests for comments.

(Reporting by Jasper Ward; editing by Susan Heavey)

data.description: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Inc. revised consolidated earnings guidance for the year ending March 31, 2026. For the year, the company expects profit attributable to owners of parent of JPY 1,500,000 million or JPY 390.39 per share.

data.title: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Inc. Announces Dividend for the Second Quarter Ended September 30, 2025, Payable on December 2, 2025 and Provides Year End Dividend for the Year Ending March 31, 2026
data.description:
Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Inc. announced dividend of JPY 78.00 per share for the second quarter ended September 30, 2025 compared to JPY 180.00 per share paid a year ago. Dividend payment date: December 2, 2025. Record date: September 30, 2025.

The company provided year end dividend for the year ending March 31, 2026. The company expects to pay year end dividend of JPY 79.00 per share for the year ending March 31, 2026 compared to JPY 62.00 per share paid a year ago.
